name: woocommerce
description: Manage WooCommerce store orders, products, and customers through the WooCommerce REST API v3

envVars.name: WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_KEY
envVars.description: WooCommerce REST API consumer key (starts with ck_), used as the HTTP Basic username

envVars.name: WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_SECRET
envVars.description: WooCommerce REST API consumer secret (starts with cs_), used as the HTTP Basic password

envVars.name: WOOCOMMERCE_STORE_DOMAIN
envVars.description: Domain of the WordPress site hosting your WooCommerce store (e.g. shop.example.com); the REST API lives at https://<domain>/wp-json/wc/v3

tools.name: List Orders
tools.description: List orders in the store with optional status, search, and date filters

status.description: Filter by order status: any, pending, processing, on-hold, completed, cancelled, refunded, failed, or trash

search.description: Limit results to orders matching a search string

after.description: Only return orders created after this ISO 8601 date, e.g. 2026-01-01T00:00:00

tools.name: Get Order
tools.description: Retrieve full details of a specific order, including line items, totals, and shipping

tools.name: Update Order Status
tools.description: Update an order's status (e.g. mark as completed) and optionally add a customer note

status.description: New order status: pending, processing, on-hold, completed, cancelled, refunded, or failed

customer_note.description: Optional note shown to the customer

tools.name: List Products
tools.description: List products in the catalog with optional search, status, and SKU filters

search.description: Limit results to products matching a search string

status.description: Filter by product status: any, draft, pending, private, or publish

sku.description: Limit results to products with a specific SKU

tools.name: Create Product
tools.description: Create a new product in the store catalog

type.description: Product type: simple, grouped, external, or variable (defaults to simple)

regular_price.description: Regular price as a decimal string, e.g. "19.99"

short_description.description: Short product description shown in listings

status.description: Product status: draft, pending, private, or publish (defaults to publish)

categories.description: Category assignments as objects with category id, e.g. [{"id": 9}]

images.description: Product images as objects with src URL, e.g. [{"src": "https://example.com/image.jpg"}]

tools.name: Get Product
tools.description: Retrieve full details of a specific product, including price, stock, and categories

tools.name: Update Product
tools.description: Update an existing product's price, stock, status, or descriptions

regular_price.description: Regular price as a decimal string, e.g. "19.99"

sale_price.description: Sale price as a decimal string; set to an empty string to remove the sale

manage_stock.description: Enable stock management at the product level (required for stock_quantity to apply)

stock_quantity.description: Stock quantity (only used when manage_stock is true)

status.description: Product status: draft, pending, private, or publish

short_description.description: Short product description shown in listings

tools.name: Get Sales Report
tools.description: Get a sales report with totals for revenue, orders, items, and customers over a period or date range

period.description: Report period: week, month, last_month, or year (defaults to today; ignored when date_min/date_max are set)

date_min.description: Return sales for a specific start date, YYYY-MM-DD

date_max.description: Return sales for a specific end date, YYYY-MM-DD

tools.name: List Customers
tools.description: List store customers with optional email, role, and search filters

email.description: Limit results to the customer with this exact email address

search.description: Limit results to customers matching a search string

role.description: Filter by user role, e.g. customer, subscriber, or all

prompts.title: Recent orders
prompts.prompt: Show me the most recent orders in my WooCommerce store and flag any that are still pending or on hold.

prompts.title: Mark order completed
prompts.prompt: Find an order by its number in my WooCommerce store and mark it as completed.

prompts.title: Catalog overview
prompts.prompt: Give me an overview of my WooCommerce product catalog, including drafts that are not yet published.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: WooCommerce setup

steps.title: Get a WooCommerce store
steps.description: You need a WordPress site with the WooCommerce plugin active and HTTPS enabled. For testing, spin up a free local site with WordPress Studio or a hosted trial (e.g. WooExpress) and install WooCommerce from Plugins > Add New.

steps.title: Open the REST API key screen
steps.description: In the WordPress admin, go to WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API and click Add key.

steps.title: Generate the key pair
steps.description: Enter a description, pick the owning user, and choose the permission level: Read for read-only access, or Read/Write if the agent should update orders and create products. Click Generate API key.

steps.title: Copy the credentials
steps.description: Copy the Consumer key (ck_...) into WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_KEY and the Consumer secret (cs_...) into WOOCOMMERCE_CONSUMER_SECRET. The secret is shown only once. Set WOOCOMMERCE_STORE_DOMAIN to your store's domain, e.g. shop.example.com.

notes: The REST API is served from your own store domain at https://<store-domain>/wp-json/wc/v3; every tool takes the store domain as the storeDomain parameter.
Keys generated with Read permission will receive 401 errors on write tools like update_order_status and create_product.
Credentials are sent via HTTP Basic auth, so the store must be served over HTTPS.
If your site uses non-pretty permalinks, the wp-json route may be unavailable; enable pretty permalinks under Settings > Permalinks.
